deferred.reason: Execution benchmarks stay deferred until parser and module-boundary harnesses exist.

notes: Phase 2 module-boundary timings use tiny import and helper-call workloads so the scorecard stays focused on public API overhead.
Phase 1 compile-path suite uses compile() as a parser proxy until a narrower benchmark hook exists.

implementation.native_unavailable_reason: built-native timing path not requested; using the default source-tree shim

notes: Collection/replacement boundary workloads keep literals and haystacks intentionally tiny so the timings stay about helper-call overhead instead of regex throughput.
Cache labels describe module compile-cache state or precompiled Pattern provenance rather than replacement-template complexity or large-haystack scanning cost.
Compile-path workloads remain the parser proxy portion of the suite until a narrower parser hook exists.
Literal-flag boundary workloads keep patterns and haystacks intentionally tiny so the timings expose API-level IGNORECASE helper-call overhead instead of regex throughput.
Supported literal-only IGNORECASE helpers are measured directly, while unsupported flag combinations stay in the same pack as explicit known-gap rows rather than disappearing from the scorecard.
Module-boundary workloads keep haystacks intentionally small so the timings emphasize public helper overhead.
Phase 2 pattern-boundary workloads keep compilation outside the timed loop so the scorecard isolates precompiled Pattern helper overhead from module helper dispatch and parser cost.
Cache labels describe the provenance of the precompiled pattern object, not large-haystack throughput or blended compile-plus-match behavior.
Phase 3 regression/stability pack keeps a curated set of parser-stress and module-boundary probes tied to the tracked specs.
These workloads are intentionally small and explicitly labeled so the scorecard can flag regressions without pretending to measure regex-engine throughput.
